preamble: The following is current environmental context for awareness only. It does not modify expertise, principles, or project commitments. Use it to inform recommendations — never to revise fundamentals.

ops.current_summary: Environmental awareness only — not doctrinal. FUNDING: April 2026 carried Q1's record AI-funded momentum forward — month-level reporting cites 1,314 VC deals with AI continuing to dominate. Seed-stage AI companies are commanding ~42% valuation premiums; median pre-money for AI seed is now near $17.9M. SoloBuilder.ai's 22 April 2026 data-driven guide documents the standardised solopreneur stack at $300-500/month operating cost, $3-12K annually — a 95-98% cost reduction vs 2020 baselines. SOLO FOUNDER TOOLING: Solo-founded startup share has risen from 23.7% (2019) to 36.3% (mid-2025); industry commentary increasingly treats AI-native solo founders as mainstream rather than novelty. Cursor, Claude Code, Lovable, n8n and Replit remain canonical. REGULATION: The EU AI Act Digital Omnibus political agreement is targeted for 28 April 2026 in Strasbourg — tomorrow. The agreement would extend high-risk AI deadlines to 2 December 2027 (standalone) and 2 August 2028 (embedded), retain watermarking obligations at 2 November 2026, and add a ban on AI-generated non-consensual intimate imagery to Article 5. In the US, the White House released a National Policy Framework for AI on 20 March 2026. Colorado's March 2026 working group draft would reset the Colorado AI Act's effective date from 30 June 2026 to 1 January 2027. RISKS: Vercel's 19 April customer-credential breach (see Tech) reaches founders using the platform. EU watermarking obligations (November 2026) are firmer than the high-risk extensions. OPPORTUNITIES: Continuing solo-founder normalisation in industry coverage validates the SageReasoning operating model. The proposed Article 5 expansion on non-consensual intimate imagery aligns directionally with R17 protections.

tech.current_summary: Environmental awareness only — not doctrinal. CLAUDE: Opus 4.7 details have continued to surface since the 16 April release. Notable: high-resolution image support up to 2576px / 3.75MP (vs prior 1568px / 1.15MP); 'task budgets' giving Claude a target token estimate for full agentic loops; a new tokenizer that may use 1.0–1.35x more tokens than Opus 4.6 (cost-modelling implication). Anthropic added a Rate Limits API for programmatic quota inspection and launched Managed Agents in public beta (managed-agents-2026-04-01 beta header) — a fully managed harness with sandboxing and SSE streaming. The 1M token context window beta for Sonnet 4.5 / Sonnet 4 retires on 30 April 2026; Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.6 support 1M tokens at standard pricing without a beta header. VERCEL — INCIDENT: A 19 April 2026 breach at Vercel originated from a February Lumma Stealer infection at third-party AI tool Context.ai. The attacker pivoted via an OAuth token to a Vercel employee's Google Workspace, then enumerated and decrypted non-sensitive environment variables. Vercel says 'hundreds of users across many organisations' may be affected; ShinyHunters claimed responsibility but the group denied involvement. Vercel published incident-response guidance recommending credential rotation. TYPESCRIPT: TypeScript 7.0 Beta released 21 April 2026 — Go-based native compiler ('Corsa'), ~10x faster than 6.0; project load times ~8x faster. Type-checking is structurally identical; clean 6.0 code should compile identically. LLM COSTS: April comparisons place Sonnet 4.6 at $3/$15, Opus 4.7 at $5/$25; commodity LLM pricing fell ~80% over twelve months. RISKS: The Vercel breach is the most material item this week — credential audit and rotation warranted for any environment-variable secrets stored on the platform. OPPORTUNITIES: Managed Agents simplifies harness work; TypeScript 7 promises material build-time wins.

growth.current_summary: Environmental awareness only — not doctrinal. STOIC COMMUNITY: April is Meditations Month at Daily Stoic — a global community read marking Marcus Aurelius's birthday, with a live Q&A from Ryan Holiday available for purchasers before 26 April. The Stoic Arts Conference at Tufts University (with Modern Stoicism) ran this month — the first modern event bringing scholars, artists, musicians and writers together around 'Stoic arts', reinforcing the cultural-practice framing of the 2026 resurgence rather than the productivity-hacks framing. AI AGENT ECOSYSTEM: Enterprise AI-agent investment has crossed $600B; Google Cloud Next 2026 emphasised the A2A (agent-to-agent) protocol and Workspace Studio, signalling protocol-layer competition with Anthropic and OpenAI. ServiceNow's agent marketplace continues to mature. April commentary increasingly positions agents as 'infrastructure' rather than experiment. COMPETITORS: Virtue AI (Bo Li, Sanmi Koyejo, Dawn Song, Carlos Guestrin) was named a Representative Vendor in the 2026 Gartner Market Guide for Guardian Agents, with $30M raised. Its product line — VirtueRed (red-teaming across 320+ risk categories), VirtueGuard (guardrail models), Agent ForgingGround (continuous stress-testing) — operates in adjacent territory. The category remains oriented to security/compliance and bias auditing; no competitor visibly couples virtue ethics with agent assessment. DEVREL / CONTENT: Stack Overflow's 2025 survey shows 84% of developers use or plan to use AI tools (up from 76%); enterprise LLM-API spend has more than doubled to ~$8.4B. Machine-readable content for AI search remains the dominant 2026 marketing meme. RISKS: Virtue AI's brand and 'agent assessment' framing could create category confusion despite different ethical foundations. Agent-marketplace fragmentation continues. OPPORTUNITIES: The Tufts cultural-practice framing and the persistent absence of virtue-ethics competitors continue to favour SageReasoning's distinctive position.

support.current_summary: Environmental awareness only — not doctrinal. ACCESSIBILITY — MATERIAL CHANGE: On 20 April 2026 the US Department of Justice issued an Interim Final Rule extending the ADA Title II web accessibility compliance deadline. The 24 April 2026 deadline for state/local entities of 50,000+ moved to 26 April 2027; entities under 50,000 or special districts now have until 26 April 2028. WCAG 2.1 AA remains the technical standard. SageReasoning is not a covered entity, but the industry benchmark now has a longer practical horizon — and the standard is unchanged. MENTAL HEALTH TECH: The American Medical Association sent a letter to Congress this month calling for safeguards on AI mental health chatbots, citing cases of chatbot-encouraged suicide and self-harm. ECRI ranked AI chatbot misuse in healthcare as the top 2026 health-technology hazard. A JMIR Mental Health rapid scoping review documented psychiatric adverse events from generative AI chatbots. Brown University research found AI chatbots systematically violate mental-health ethics standards — even when prompted to use evidence-based techniques. AI SAFETY: Stanford's 2026 AI Index (423 pages) — documented AI incidents rose to 362 (up from 233 in 2024); OECD's Incident Monitor peaked at 435 monthly in January 2026. Organisations rating incident response 'excellent' fell from 28% to 18% in a year; 62% now cite security/risk as the #1 blocker to scaling agentic AI. PUBLIC SENTIMENT: Gen Z AI excitement fell from 36% (2025) to 22% (2026); 'angry' rose from 22% to 31%. RISKS — FOR FOUNDER ATTENTION: (1) The AMA intervention raises the regulatory tempo on mental-health AI — directly relevant to R20a. (2) Brown's 'systematic violation' finding sets a high bar for any companion-style interaction. OPPORTUNITIES: Cooling Gen Z sentiment sustains the case for honest-positioning operators; Utah's safe-harbor model remains the closest regulatory analogue to R20.
